capability_objective: The communications lead is auditing two outbound-message entries before the weekly send review. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the message named “Storm Closure Advisory” at “Campus Operations” and the message named “Library Hours Correction” at “Student Services”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Report only each record’s name, stable ID, location, date, and status, followed by one status-comparison sentence.
prompt:
The communications lead is auditing two outbound-message entries before the weekly send review. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `message-audit.txt` from full message records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `MESSAGES.md` and use the genuine local `./messagectl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the message named “Storm Closure Advisory” at “Campus Operations” and the message named “Library Hours Correction” at “Student Services”. Your first message-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./messagectl` processes, with no other message operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following message-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./messagectl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries. Take the date and status only from a successful full-record get.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter message data. Do not expose unrelated records, message bodies, recipients, or other extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `message-audit.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`message-audit.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank lines with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. Keep the two records in the requested order. Format each resolved line as `<name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status>`. For an unresolved branch, use `<requested name> | UNRESOLVED | <requested location> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED`. The third line must be exactly `Status comparison: same (<status>).` when both retrieved statuses match, `Status comparison: different (<first status> vs <second status>).` when they differ, or `Status comparison: unavailable.` when either branch is unresolved. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
